title: desmodium micranthum
intro: Desmodium micranthum, also known as Mexican tea or false indigo, is a perennial herb native to Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Desmodium micranthum include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and made into teas or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
